name: Soy Vey! Challah (Bread)
recipeIngredient: 1 Egg yolk (you can use egg substitute if you prefer)
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix the soy milk, honey, and yeast in a bowl. Let it sit for 10 minutes.
2. In a large bowl, combine bread flour and soy flour.
3. Add oil, eggs, and egg yolk to the soy milk mixture. Stir until well combined.
4. Gradually add the flours to the liquid mixture. Mix with a wooden spoon until a dough forms.
5. Turn the dough out onto a floured surface. Knead until smooth and elastic (about 10 minutes).
6. Form into a ball, lightly coat with oil, and place in a covered bowl.
7. Let rise until doubled in size, about an hour.
8. Punch down the dough and knead for 3 minutes. Shape into desired form (braided or otherwise).
9. Place on a greased baking sheet. Cover and let rise until doubled, about 45 minutes.
10. Preheat oven to 375°F.
11. Beat egg white with a fork. Brush over the top of the loaf.
12. Sprinkle poppy seeds over the loaf. Bake at 375°F for 20 minutes.
13. Cool on a rack.
Baking temperature: 375°F
Baking time: 20 minutes
Yield: One large loaf
This recipe won first place in a special competition at the Ohio State Fair this year, sponsored by the Ohio Soybean Council. It also received an "award of Excellence" rosette. You can also use this recipe in a bread machine using the dough setting or shape it into a braid before baking. If you're not fond of soy, replace soy milk with water and soy oil with another type of oil.
